# Title: Information required to sell on Amazon Brazil

Title: Information required to sell on Amazon Brazil

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201200780

Markdown Content:
## Provide your information to Amazon Serviços de Varejo do Brasil Ltda

In accordance with Brazilian regulations, Amazon Serviços de Varejo do Brasil Ltda (ASV) requires sellers to provide information about themselves and their business so that they can receive the proceeds of their transactions on Amazon stores.

To provide the required information, sign in to Seller Central using the email address and password associated with your account. In order to provide this information, select the **Settings**, and then **Account info**. From your seller account information page, click each section that is highlighted in red. This information will be used to verify your selling on Amazon account. We will contact you if any additional information is required. Note that this is a normal process and aims to bring safety to our customers.

ASV knows that you care how information about you is used and shared. We appreciate your trust that we will do so carefully and sensibly.

**Note:**Shipments from Brazil can only be done if the business entity is registered in Brazil.

## Compliance

#### Why do I have to provide this information?

The verification of your identity is required by Brazilian laws and regulations and is a safety measure for our customers. You may need to provide us with certain information or documents as requested in order to facilitate these checks.

#### In how much time do I have to provide the information?

You can provide the required verification information at any time after you have registered. You may have limited access to your account until the information has been provided and verified.

Account information is subject to re-verification periodically or if your account information is updated. This is also mandatory under Brazilian legislation and regulation.

Amazon allows up to 60 days for you to complete re-verification. This does not include the time we take to complete the review. Your ability to sell and disburse funds may be restricted if re-verification is not completed within 60 days.

When we require information, a notification will appear on your [account information page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/on-board/configuration/index.html). We may provide additional information about the request in your [performance notifications](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/performance/notifications).

#### What will happen if I don't provide the required information?

You will eventually be unable to use your selling on Amazon account. Therefore, you will not be able to sell on Amazon if the information is not provided.

#### What happens if I don't want to accept the business agreements?

#### What happens if I don't want to accept the business agreements?

Accepting the Amazon user agreement is a condition of registering for the service. If you do not agree to these terms, you will not be able to register for an Amazon seller account, which will result in removal of your selling privileges on Amazon Brasil.

#### What is the difference between a Private person (Pessoa física) and a Business (Pessoa jurídica)?

New sellers:

If you have a business and have a registered CNPJ, register yourself as a Business (Pessoa jurídica), even if you are a sole proprietor, microbusiness entity or unipersonal society.

If you do not have a registered CNPJ, you can register yourself using your CPF as an Individual (Pessoa física).

Make sure that you choose the appropriate option in the drop-down menu to ensure the best registering experience.

Make sure that you choose the appropriate option in the drop-down menu to ensure the best registering experience.

At this time, sellers do not have the capability to change their legal entity in the account page, so if you register the wrong legal entity type, your registration process will be declined and Amazon will request you to start a new registration process. We will let you know when changing legal entity in your account page becomes available.

Out-of-country sellers that already sell in other Amazon stores:

Your entity type will be the same as the one you first registered to sell in another Amazon store (either Individual or Business seller).

#### What is meant by "a legal representative of the business"?

#### What is meant by "a legal representative of the business"?

A legal representative is a person who is authorized by your business to act on its behalf (for instance, accept Terms & Conditions and open an account). The representative may be an owner of the business, an executive officer, or someone who has the specific powers to act on behalf of the business.

**Note:**Amazon will need to confirm if the person indicated has the power to act on behalf of the entity, so we advise to make sure that the official documentation matches the one you send to Amazon. This is a safety measure to ensure that companies will not have their data used for non-authentic purposes and that only authorized personnel have access to the account.

#### How is the primary contact person defined?

#### How is the primary contact person defined?

The primary contact person is the person who has access to the selling account. They provide the registration information on behalf of the account holder (the registered seller) and initiate transactions such as disbursements and refunds. Actions taken by the primary contact person are deemed to be taken by the account holder.

If the primary contact person is not the beneficial owner or the legal representative of the business, a letter of authorization ("LOA") signed by a legal representative will be requested, confirming that the primary contact person is authorized to act on behalf of the business.

#### What is expected to be in a letter of authorization for the point of contact?

A letter of authorization is required in order for your account to be eligible for a seller account if you are not the legal representative of the business. We expect to receive a statement like the one below or something comparable:

[letterhead of the company]

POWER TO ACT ON BEHALF OF THE COMPANY

The undersigned [name of Company here] (herein after, the “Company”), duly represented by (name, date of birth, and function/role/title) [add full name, date of birth, ID, CPF, full address and function of the signatory here], confirms that [add full name of the Point of Contact here] is authorized to open a Seller account, accept the Amazon user agreement and other Amazon policies, have access to the Seller account, download statements, and initiate transactions in the name and on behalf of the Company.

Dated this:

Signed by:

#### How is the beneficial owner defined?

Dated this:

Signed by:

#### How is the beneficial owner defined?

A beneficial owner is a natural person who (directly or indirectly) owns 25% or more of the shares or voting rights of the company. If such a natural person does not exist, then provide information of a natural person who exercises ultimate control over the management of the company. If no such natural person exists, then provide information of a natural person who controls the company or who holds the position of senior managing official.

The following questions can help you determine the person who controls the company:

The following questions can help you determine the person who controls the company:

1.   Are there individuals who control the company in fact, directly or indirectly?
2.   Are there individuals who have the power to appoint or remove members of administrative, management or supervisory bodies, directly or indirectly?
3.   Are there individuals who are responsible for key managerial decisions, directly or indirectly?
4.   Are there individuals who can use all or part of the assets of the company?

Ensure that all the information (such as phone number) associated with the beneficial owners is accurate, and get identity proof documents ready to expedite the process.

You can provide up to four beneficial owner names. If there are more than four beneficial owners, we'll get this additional information from you later.

#### How do I add beneficial owners to my account?

#### How do I add beneficial owners to my account?

In case the beneficial owner is not the same person as the primary contact person, you have to provide details for them and each of the beneficiary owners of the company meeting the above criteria. Where there are no beneficial owners meeting the above criteria, a senior manager may be required to be entered on the account in place of a beneficial owner.

To update the number of beneficial owners, follow these steps in Seller Central:

1.   Go to **Identity information**.
2.   Click **Add beneficial owners**, located at the bottom of the screen.
3.   Enter the beneficial owners' details.
4.   Repeat the above steps for all applicable beneficial owners.
5.   Click **Submit for verification**.

## Information collection

## What information am I required to provide?

## Information collection

## What information am I required to provide?

Identity information for Individuals (Pessoas físicas): this is a safety measure to ensure that individuals will not have their data used for non-authentic purposes, and that the person indicated really has authorization to have access to the account.

Documents accepted:

*   Full valid passport
*   National identity card
*   Driver's license
*   Residence permit
*   Work history booklet
*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)
*   Passport

The document must meet the following criteria:

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be a copy of a government issued ID document with a photo
*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date. In case the document does not have an expiration date, consider the expiration date any date in the future.

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Bank statement
*   Certified bank letter (CBL)
*   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
*   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent
*   Residence confirmation
*   Building society or credit union statement
*   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
*   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency
*   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits
*   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks 
**Note:**This type of document sometimes does not have the address information.

Ensure that the proof of address you send is recent (it is mandatory to be dated from the past 180 days) and have your name and information on it.

Lastly, Amazon will also request a letter of authorization in order for your account to be eligible for a seller account if you are a registered point of contact, but not the legal representative of the business.

Amazon will request documentation to verify identity of:

Amazon will request documentation to verify identity of:

*   Individual sellers
*   Primary contact person of business sellers: person authorized to manage seller account
*   Secondary user of business sellers: other users also authorized to manage seller account
*   Shareholders and partners of business sellers: individuals who own the company
*   Beneficial owners of business sellers: individuals who are considered the beneficial owners of the company, according to the Receita Federal criteria
*   Individuals who, ultimately, directly or indirectly, own, control, or significantly influence the entity (that is, own more than 25% of entity's share capital or voting rights and, acting individually or jointly, hold or exercise preponderance in corporate deliberations and have the power to elect the majority of the entity's administrators, even without controlling it), or to whose behalf a transaction is conducted

## Joint stock company (SA)

Documents accepted for the verification of legal entity type:

## Joint stock company (SA)

Documents accepted for the verification of legal entity type:

*   Simplified Certificate 
*   Articles of Association/Statute 
*   Registration extract

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must indicate name of the ONG, address registered in Receita Federal, Business Purpose (CNAE), Type of entity (SA), and shareholders and final beneficiaries who own the company and who benefit from it, following Receita Federal's criteria.

Documents accepted for the identification of individuals related to entity:

*   Full valid passport
*   National identity card (RG)
*   Driver's license (CNH). Digital CNH is also accepted, but must contain the QR code (extract the driver’s license as a PDF in the official app).
*   Residence permit (RNE, RNM, or CRNM)
*   Work history booklet (original CTPS, and not the app version)
*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be a copy of a government issued ID document with a photo
*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Payslip or invoice issued by a public agency
*   Phone bill
*   Internet bill
*   Utility bill
*   Credit card bill. The document must have the address information. 
**Note:**This type of document sometimes does not have the address information.

Ensure that the proof of address you send is recent (it is mandatory to be dated from the past 180 days) and have the name of the SA in it.

#### Where can I get a copy of my business registration extract?

You can request a copy of your company's registration extract online.

#### Where can I get a copy of my business registration extract?

You can request a copy of your company's registration extract online.

#### I entered some information and now the items listed in red changed. Why?

This means that Amazon needs more information to verify your identity. In some cases, additional identity information is required for your Amazon account, to comply with applicable legislation and regulation. Alternatively, you may have provided some information which we were unable to verify, so we need you to enter this portion of the information again. This is a safety measure to our customers.

#### How do I provide this information?

In order to provide the requested information, go to the **Settings** tab of your Seller Central account, and then click **Account Info**. From the **Seller Account Information** page, click each section that is highlighted in red.

#### How can I upload more than one document at a time?

#### How can I upload more than one document at a time?

Sometimes, we may require several documents from you and this number may be greater than the number of fields present in Seller Central for you to upload the documents. If this is the case, you have to merge the documents into one document and upload it to Seller Central to review. In order to do this, you can follow one of the two methods explained below that we would recommend you to do:

1.   You can merge the scanned images into a Microsoft Word document and create a PDF document. The easiest way to do this is to insert JPEG images into the word document and then create the PDF document while saving the file as explained below:
    *   Click **Insert** on the toolbar at the top of Microsoft Word.
    *   Click **Pictures** and locate the file that you want to add to the document.
    *   Repeat the steps above with all the images that you want to add to the document.
    *   Make sure that all the images are on separate pages.
    *   Click **File** and select **Save As** and select “PDF” as the document type to save.

2.   You can also use Adobe Acrobat to create a PDF.

Alternatively, while scanning the documents, you can opt to create a PDF document and scan the pages to create a single document.

#### What if I have to update my information?

#### What if I have to update my information?

From the **Settings** tab, click **Account info** and then, from the **Seller Account Information** page, click each section that you want to update. If your information has already been verified or the verification process has not yet started, you will be able to update your information. Any updates are subject to verification. Depending on our verification results, you may be asked to provide additional information.

#### Why can't I edit my information?

Information cannot be updated while it is being verified.

#### Can I save my information and complete the form later?

Yes. The Update Wizard will allow you to resume entering data at any step in the process. Make sure that you click **Submit** on each screen after entering your information.

You will still be able to use Seller Central. However, if you do not provide your information within the specified time period, your selling privileges will be suspended. Your privileges may be reinstated once you have entered the requested data and your information have been verified.

#### I provided the information requested. Why has Amazon requested additional information from me?

During the verification process, we may determine that you are required to provide additional information. This information is required to complete your account registration and for safety purposes.

#### How will I be notified if Amazon requires additional information?

If we require additional information, you will be notified via the email address provided at registration as well as via the notification field in Seller Central. You can also see what additional information is required by clicking **Account Info** on the **Settings** tab. Look for the sections highlighted in red.

You will also receive detailed guidance in your [performance notifications](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/performance/notifications).

#### What if my country of residence isn't in the drop-down menu?

If your country of residence is not in the drop-down menu, we don't support that country. Check back often; we're continually adding supported countries.

#### What should I do if I am unable to upload my documents using the tool provided?

Contact [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/hz/contact-us/) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant if you encounter problems while uploading documents.

#### I don't have one of the documents requested. What should I do?

#### I don't have one of the documents requested. What should I do?

If you don’t have one or more of the documents requested, contact [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/cu/contact-us) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant. Make sure that you provide details about the documents you do not have when you contact us or use Amazon's Seller Assistant. We will then get in touch with you regarding alternative documents that we may be able to accept.

#### Why do my documents need to be dated within a certain timeline?

Because Amazon is required by regulation and legislation that identification is done based on up to date information. This information is required to complete your account registration and for safety purposes.

#### What language should I enter the information in?

You can provide the information in Portuguese, Chinese, or English.

## If your business is registered in the United States of America

*   **United States Incorporated (Inc. or Corp.) companies.** You may be requested to provide one or more of the following documents:
    *   Articles or Certificate of Incorporation, Formation or Organization: This must be issued once to the company
    *   Certificate of Good Standing: This can be issued on request by the State Secretary’s Office - this document must be dated from the past 180 days. [Links to obtain Certificate of Good Standing by State](https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/help/hub/reference/external/GMN9F5MCYH2MD82E?ref=efph_201200780_cont_901&locale=en-GB). 
    *   Signed By-Laws. This document should be signed and dated by all Beneficial owners and include rules on the corporation's objectives, its management structure, and its internal processes. If you do not have one, you can create Bylaws online: [https://www.lawdepot.com/contracts/corporate-bylaws/](https://www.lawdepot.com/contracts/corporate-bylaws/)

*   Utility bill, bank statement, or credit card statement in the name of the business and dated from the past 180 days. 
    *   Certified bank letter (CBL)
    *   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
    *   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent
    *   Residence confirmation
    *   Building society or credit union statement
    *   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
    *   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency
    *   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits
    *   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks

*   **US Limited Liability Companies.** You may be requested to provide one or more of the following documents:
    *   Articles or Certificate of Incorporation, Formation or Organization: This must be issued once to the company
    *   Certificate of Good Standing: This certificate can be issued on request by the State Secretary’s Office - this document must be dated from the past 180 days. [Links to obtain Certificate of Good Standing by State](https://sellercentral.amazon.co.uk/help/hub/reference/external/GMN9F5MCYH2MD82E?ref=efph_201200780_cont_901&locale=en-GB)
    *   Operating Agreement. This document should include the total number of company’s shares, shareholding percentage of all business owners, the procedure for transferring shares, and the date and signatures of all shareholders. If you do not have one, you can create an Operating Agreement online: [https://eforms.com/operating-agreements/](https://eforms.com/operating-agreements/)

*   Utility bill, bank statement, or credit card statement in the name of the business and dated from the past 180 days. Certified bank letter (CBL)
    *   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
    *   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent
    *   Residence confirmation
    *   Building society or credit union statement
    *   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
    *   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency
    *   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits
    *   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks

*   **US Sole Proprietors.** You may be requested to provide one or more of the following documents:
    *   Fictitious Business Name Certificate: This is issued only once to the seller
    *   Doing Business document
    *   Document from Internal Revenue Service (IRS): To prove that you are registered for paying tax on their earnings - this would most likely be a Tax Return
    *   Utility bill, bank statement, or credit card statement in the name of the sole proprietor or the trading name and dated from the past 180 days. 
    *   Certified bank letter (CBL)
    *   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
    *   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent
    *   Residence confirmation
    *   Building society or credit union statement
    *   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
    *   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency

*   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
    *   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency
    *   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits
    *   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**Warning: We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks

*   **General Partnership.** You may be requested to provide one or more of the following documents:

    *   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits

**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks

*   **Registered Charity in the United States.** You must provide all of the following documents at the time of registration:

## If your business is registered in Taiwan

*   Business Registration
Private limited companies must provide one of the following:

    *   Private limited company registration form 有限公司登記表

If the registration extract document was issued more than one year ago, a copy of the business owner confirmation signed by a legal representative must be submitted.

Private limited joint-stock companies must provide one of the following:

Private limited joint-stock companies must provide one of the following:

## If you are an individual seller

We may ask you to provide proof of address.

**Proof of address**

*   Proof of address must meet the following requirements:
    *   Be dated within the past 180 days
    *   Show the name and the address of the individual
    *   The address entered in Seller Central and the address in the document must match. If the ID document has the address, no additional proof of address is required.
    *   Cannot be handwritten
    *   Cannot be a screenshot

*   Accepted documents include, but are not limited to, the following:
    *   Utility bill (example: water, electricity, gas) in the name of the individual
    *   Bank statement or credit card statement in the name of the individual
    *   Certified Bank Letter in the name of the individual
    *   Income tax bill
    *   Social Security receipt
    *   Bill from estate or property management in the name of the individual
    *   Bank certified customer printout in the name of the individual
    *   Phone bill in the name of the individual
    *   VAT invoice in the name of the individual
**Note:**Documents from financial service providers or online digital banks are acceptable as proof of address from institutions authorized by the PBC Bank of China.

**Identity**

**Identity**

*   Mainland China:
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Valid Residence Identity Card (both sides)
    *   Hukou (pages related to the Business Owner, Legal Representative or Primary Contact, including at least a name, address, ID number)

*   Hong Kong:
    *   Document of Identity for Visa Purposes (Photo and information page)
    *   Hong Kong ID Card (front side) only if the place of birth is Hong Kong, Mainland China, or Macau

*   Taiwan:
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Taiwan Identity Card (front and reverse sides)

## Contact Information

**Information about primary contact person**

The primary contact person (POC) is the main person who has access to the selling account, provides the registration information on behalf of the account holder (the registered seller), and initiates transactions, such as disbursements and refunds. You will be asked to provide contact details of the POC.

Accepted identity documents:

Accepted identity documents:

*   Mainland China
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Valid Residence Identity Card (both sides)
    *   Hukou (pages related to the Business Owner, Legal Representative or Primary Contact, including at least a name, address, ID number)

*   Hong Kong
    *   Document of Identity for Visa Purposes (Photo and information page)
    *   Hong Kong ID Card (front side) only if the place of birth is Hong Kong, Mainland China, or Macau

*   Taiwan
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Taiwan Identity Card (front and reverse sides)

**Additional information**

Bank statements from financial service providers or online digital banks are acceptable as proof of address from institutions authorized by the PBC Bank of China.

List: ANNA, Fire, Revolut, Banca Widiba, First Direct, Saxo Payments, Banking Circle, Hype, Selfyconto, CashPlus, Meridan Solutions, Solaris Bank, Chebanca!, Mettle, Starling Bank, Coconut, Monzo, Thinkmoney, CountingUp, N26, Tide, Ebury, Nexi, Webank, EVO Banco, Paysera, Qonto, Fineco, and Penta

**Information about beneficiaries, directors, or trustees**

According to Brazilian regulations, any person holding 25 percent or more of a company's shares or equity is considered an ultimate beneficial owner (UBO). As a result, you may be required to provide information about these individuals. This requirement also extends to trustees and directors.

To verify ownership, you can provide a document that contains the share allotment of the company per owner.

For identification purposes , Amazon may request a passport or Identity Card, along with proof of address documentation.

**Note:**The primary contact person may also be a beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the company.

**Note:**The primary contact person may also be a beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the company.

For more information, see the [Brazil requirements](https://www.gov.br/coaf/pt-br/acesso-a-informacao/Institucional/a-atividade-de-supervisao/regulacao/supervisao/normas-1/resolucao-coaf-no-36-de-10-de-marco-de-2021).

If the appointed POC is a beneficial owner, director, or trustee, but there is another beneficial owner or beneficial owners of the business, you must provide information about all other beneficial owners with 25% or more ownership of the business.

If the primary contact person is not the beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the business, a letter of authorization (LOA) signed by a beneficial owner, director, or trustee may be requested confirming that the primary contact person is authorized to act on behalf of the business. In the case where the POC is the Legal Representative for a seller, a LOA is not required.

## Information Validation

## Information Validation

## How long will it take to verify my information?

Once we receive all of the required information, we will proceed with the verification of your account. Verification could take up to 3 days; however, it may take longer in some situations based on the complexity of the account.

## Am I allowed to sell while my information is being verified?

You will not be able to start selling on Amazon until the information that you have provided has been verified and your account has successfully completed verification.

## How can I appeal Amazon's decision to decline my application?

Verification decisions are final and are based on the information and documentation we have received from you.

## Impact on selling on Amazon

## What happens while my information is being verified?

## Impact on selling on Amazon

## What happens while my information is being verified?

You may be allowed to start selling on Amazon while the information you have provided at registration is being verified, subject to certain limits. Depending on certain criteria, we may require that you provide us with all the required information and that this information is verified before we open your selling account. In this case, you will not be able to start selling on Amazon until the information you have provided is verified and your account has successfully completed verification.

## What happens to my orders if I have not provided the required identity information or if my information is pending verification?

## What happens to my orders if I have not provided the required identity information or if my information is pending verification?

If you have been allowed to start selling on Amazon immediately after registration, you can fulfill your existing orders while your information is pending verification, up to a certain limit. However, you must provide the required identity information as soon as possible, to avoid any constraints regarding your selling account.

## Why has my application for a selling account been declined?

## Why has my application for a selling account been declined?

There are several reasons why we may have declined to open your account. It may be because we were unable to verify your identity as required by applicable legislation. Providing incomplete or inaccurate information is a common reason why verification may not be successful. If you feel your information should have been successfully verified and would like to appeal, [contact Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/contact-us/contact-amazon-form.html) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant.

## What will happen to my FBA inventory?

In the event that you are unable to open your selling account, you can ask to have your inventory returned to you.

## What should I do if I have a document that is not in any of the Amazon-supported Languages?

You have to submit officially translated documents if they are not in any of the Amazon supported languages (Portuguese, English, or Chinese).

You have to submit officially translated documents if they are not in any of the Amazon supported languages (Portuguese, English, or Chinese).

## Why doesn't Amazon Brasil support my country of residence?

Your country of residence may not be currently supported and included in the drop-down list for various reasons, including international restrictions or our ability to process information and documents in specific languages. Check back in the future as Amazon Brasil is continuously expanding the list of supported countries.

## Does Amazon have the right to change the agreement without notice?

We are committed to maintaining a dynamic, safe and compliant store for both customers and sellers. The changes to our agreements are a necessary step toward this goal. Therefore, we are requesting that all sellers accept the agreements so that we may continue to offer both customers and sellers outstanding products and services.

## What does the Business Solutions Agreement cover?

## What does the Business Solutions Agreement cover?

The Business Solutions Agreement contains the terms and conditions for selling on Amazon, Fulfillment by Amazon, Services, and Product Ads.

## I do not want to accept the new agreements. Who can I contact to discuss changes?

The agreements have to be accepted as written. Our goal is to have a transparent service that works for both sellers and Amazon.

If you have questions on the overall process, we encourage you to contact Selling Partner Support or use Amazon's Seller Assistant, but be aware that they will be unable to waive any terms of the agreements.

Title: Information required to sell on Amazon Brazil

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201200780

Published Time: Wed, 20 May 2026 08:43:48 GMT

Markdown Content:
## Provide your information to Amazon Serviços de Varejo do Brasil Ltda

In accordance with Brazilian regulations, Amazon Serviços de Varejo do Brasil Ltda (ASV) requires sellers to provide information about themselves and their business so that they can receive the proceeds of their transactions on Amazon stores.

To provide the required information, sign in to Seller Central using the email address and password associated with your account. In order to provide this information, select the **Settings**, and then **Account info**. From your seller account information page, click each section that is highlighted in red. This information will be used to verify your selling on Amazon account. We will contact you if any additional information is required. Note that this is a normal process and aims to bring safety to our customers.

ASV knows that you care how information about you is used and shared. We appreciate your trust that we will do so carefully and sensibly.

**Note:**Shipments from Brazil can only be done if the business entity is registered in Brazil.

## Compliance

#### Why do I have to provide this information?

## Compliance

#### Why do I have to provide this information?

The verification of your identity is required by Brazilian laws and regulations and is a safety measure for our customers. You may need to provide us with certain information or documents as requested in order to facilitate these checks.

#### In how much time do I have to provide the information?

You can provide the required verification information at any time after you have registered. You may have limited access to your account until the information has been provided and verified.

Account information is subject to re-verification periodically or if your account information is updated. This is also mandatory under Brazilian legislation and regulation.

Amazon allows up to 60 days for you to complete re-verification. This does not include the time we take to complete the review. Your ability to sell and disburse funds may be restricted if re-verification is not completed within 60 days.

When we require information, a notification will appear on your [account information page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/on-board/configuration/index.html). We may provide additional information about the request in your [performance notifications](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/performance/notifications).

#### What will happen if I don't provide the required information?

You will eventually be unable to use your selling on Amazon account. Therefore, you will not be able to sell on Amazon if the information is not provided.

#### What happens if I don't want to accept the business agreements?

Accepting the Amazon user agreement is a condition of registering for the service. If you do not agree to these terms, you will not be able to register for an Amazon seller account, which will result in removal of your selling privileges on Amazon Brasil.

#### What is the difference between a Private person (Pessoa física) and a Business (Pessoa jurídica)?

New sellers:

#### What is the difference between a Private person (Pessoa física) and a Business (Pessoa jurídica)?

New sellers:

If you have a business and have a registered CNPJ, register yourself as a Business (Pessoa jurídica), even if you are a sole proprietor, microbusiness entity or unipersonal society.

If you do not have a registered CNPJ, you can register yourself using your CPF as an Individual (Pessoa física).

Make sure that you choose the appropriate option in the drop-down menu to ensure the best registering experience.

At this time, sellers do not have the capability to change their legal entity in the account page, so if you register the wrong legal entity type, your registration process will be declined and Amazon will request you to start a new registration process. We will let you know when changing legal entity in your account page becomes available.

Out-of-country sellers that already sell in other Amazon stores:

Out-of-country sellers that already sell in other Amazon stores:

Your entity type will be the same as the one you first registered to sell in another Amazon store (either Individual or Business seller).

#### What is meant by "a legal representative of the business"?

A legal representative is a person who is authorized by your business to act on its behalf (for instance, accept Terms & Conditions and open an account). The representative may be an owner of the business, an executive officer, or someone who has the specific powers to act on behalf of the business.

**Note:**Amazon will need to confirm if the person indicated has the power to act on behalf of the entity, so we advise to make sure that the official documentation matches the one you send to Amazon. This is a safety measure to ensure that companies will not have their data used for non-authentic purposes and that only authorized personnel have access to the account.

#### How is the primary contact person defined?

#### How is the primary contact person defined?

The primary contact person is the person who has access to the selling account. They provide the registration information on behalf of the account holder (the registered seller) and initiate transactions such as disbursements and refunds. Actions taken by the primary contact person are deemed to be taken by the account holder.

If the primary contact person is not the beneficial owner or the legal representative of the business, a letter of authorization ("LOA") signed by a legal representative will be requested, confirming that the primary contact person is authorized to act on behalf of the business.

#### What is expected to be in a letter of authorization for the point of contact?

A letter of authorization is required in order for your account to be eligible for a seller account if you are not the legal representative of the business. We expect to receive a statement like the one below or something comparable:

[letterhead of the company]

POWER TO ACT ON BEHALF OF THE COMPANY

The undersigned [name of Company here] (herein after, the “Company”), duly represented by (name, date of birth, and function/role/title) [add full name, date of birth, ID, CPF, full address and function of the signatory here], confirms that [add full name of the Point of Contact here] is authorized to open a Seller account, accept the Amazon user agreement and other Amazon policies, have access to the Seller account, download statements, and initiate transactions in the name and on behalf of the Company.

Dated this:

Signed by:

#### How is the beneficial owner defined?

Dated this:

Signed by:

#### How is the beneficial owner defined?

A beneficial owner is a natural person who (directly or indirectly) owns 25% or more of the shares or voting rights of the company. If such a natural person does not exist, then provide information of a natural person who exercises ultimate control over the management of the company. If no such natural person exists, then provide information of a natural person who controls the company or who holds the position of senior managing official.

The following questions can help you determine the person who controls the company:

The following questions can help you determine the person who controls the company:

1.   Are there individuals who control the company in fact, directly or indirectly?
2.   Are there individuals who have the power to appoint or remove members of administrative, management or supervisory bodies, directly or indirectly?
3.   Are there individuals who are responsible for key managerial decisions, directly or indirectly?
4.   Are there individuals who can use all or part of the assets of the company?

Ensure that all the information (such as phone number) associated with the beneficial owners is accurate, and get identity proof documents ready to expedite the process.

You can provide up to four beneficial owner names. If there are more than four beneficial owners, we'll get this additional information from you later.

#### How do I add beneficial owners to my account?

#### How do I add beneficial owners to my account?

In case the beneficial owner is not the same person as the primary contact person, you have to provide details for them and each of the beneficiary owners of the company meeting the above criteria. Where there are no beneficial owners meeting the above criteria, a senior manager may be required to be entered on the account in place of a beneficial owner.

To update the number of beneficial owners, follow these steps in Seller Central:

1.   Go to **Identity information**.
2.   Click **Add beneficial owners**, located at the bottom of the screen.
3.   Enter the beneficial owners' details.
4.   Repeat the above steps for all applicable beneficial owners.
5.   Click **Submit for verification**.

## Information collection

## What information am I required to provide?

## Information collection

## What information am I required to provide?

Identity information for Individuals (Pessoas físicas): this is a safety measure to ensure that individuals will not have their data used for non-authentic purposes, and that the person indicated really has authorization to have access to the account.

Documents accepted:

*   Full valid passport
*   National identity card
*   Driver's license
*   Residence permit
*   Work history booklet
*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)
*   Passport

The document must meet the following criteria:

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be a copy of a government issued ID document with a photo
*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date. In case the document does not have an expiration date, consider the expiration date any date in the future.

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Bank statement
*   Certified bank letter (CBL)
*   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
*   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent
*   Residence confirmation
*   Building society or credit union statement
*   Mortgage statement from a bank or financial institution
*   Tenancy bill from a local council or reputable lending agency
*   Recent letter from a benefits agency such as Department for Works and Pensions, Job Centre Plus, benefits agency, or veteran's agency confirming the rights to benefits
*   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks 
**Note:**This type of document sometimes does not have the address information.

Ensure that the proof of address you send is recent (it is mandatory to be dated from the past 180 days) and have your name and information on it.

Lastly, Amazon will also request a letter of authorization in order for your account to be eligible for a seller account if you are a registered point of contact, but not the legal representative of the business.

Amazon will request documentation to verify identity of:

Amazon will request documentation to verify identity of:

*   Individual sellers
*   Primary contact person of business sellers: person authorized to manage seller account
*   Secondary user of business sellers: other users also authorized to manage seller account
*   Shareholders and partners of business sellers: individuals who own the company
*   Beneficial owners of business sellers: individuals who are considered the beneficial owners of the company, according to the Receita Federal criteria
*   Individuals who, ultimately, directly or indirectly, own, control, or significantly influence the entity (that is, own more than 25% of entity's share capital or voting rights and, acting individually or jointly, hold or exercise preponderance in corporate deliberations and have the power to elect the majority of the entity's administrators, even without controlling it), or to whose behalf a transaction is conducted

## Sole proprietor

## Sole proprietor

Documents accepted for the identification of individuals related to entity:

*   Full valid passport
*   National identity card
*   Driver's license
*   Residence permit
*   Work history booklet
*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)
*   Passport

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be a copy of a government issued ID document with a photo
*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Credit card statement or bill 
**Warning:**We do not accept prepaid credit card statements or bills.

*   Documents from the third-party providers, acceptable third-party institutions, and online banks

## Unipersonal Limited Company

Documents accepted for the verification of legal entity type:

## Unipersonal Limited Company

*   Simplified Certificate 
*   Articles of Association/Statute 
*   Registration extract

The document must meet the following criteria:

Documents accepted for the identification of individuals related to entity:

*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)

The document must meet the following criteria:

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Bank statement
*   Certified bank letter (CBL)
*   Gas, water, electricity, TV, Internet, mobile phone, or landline phone bills
*   Rent receipt from a local council or letting agent

## Joint stock company (SA)

Documents accepted for the verification of legal entity type:

*   Simplified Certificate 
*   Articles of Association/Statute 
*   Registration extract

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must indicate name of the ONG, address registered in Receita Federal, Business Purpose (CNAE), Type of entity (SA), and shareholders and final beneficiaries who own the company and who benefit from it, following Receita Federal's criteria.

Documents accepted for the identification of individuals related to entity:

*   Full valid passport
*   National identity card (RG)
*   Driver's license (CNH). Digital CNH is also accepted, but must contain the QR code (extract the driver’s license as a PDF in the official app).
*   Residence permit (RNE, RNM, or CRNM)
*   Work history booklet (original CTPS, and not the app version)
*   Identity cards issued by official supervisory bodies for professional practice (such as OAB, CRECI, and others)

The document must meet the following criteria:

*   The document must be a copy of a government issued ID document with a photo
*   The document must be in date and valid
*   The document must contain full name, date of birth, and country of citizenship
*   The document must contain an ID number and the expiration date

Additionally, you may be requested to provide copy of a proof of address document, such as:

*   Payslip or invoice issued by a public agency
*   Phone bill
*   Internet bill
*   Utility bill
*   Credit card bill. The document must have the address information. 
**Note:**This type of document sometimes does not have the address information.

Ensure that the proof of address you send is recent (it is mandatory to be dated from the past 180 days) and have the name of the SA in it.

#### Where can I get a copy of my business registration extract?

You can request a copy of your company's registration extract online.

#### Where can I get a copy of my business registration extract?

You can request a copy of your company's registration extract online.

#### I entered some information and now the items listed in red changed. Why?

This means that Amazon needs more information to verify your identity. In some cases, additional identity information is required for your Amazon account, to comply with applicable legislation and regulation. Alternatively, you may have provided some information which we were unable to verify, so we need you to enter this portion of the information again. This is a safety measure to our customers.

#### How do I provide this information?

In order to provide the requested information, go to the **Settings** tab of your Seller Central account, and then click **Account Info**. From the **Seller Account Information** page, click each section that is highlighted in red.

#### How can I upload more than one document at a time?

#### How can I upload more than one document at a time?

Sometimes, we may require several documents from you and this number may be greater than the number of fields present in Seller Central for you to upload the documents. If this is the case, you have to merge the documents into one document and upload it to Seller Central to review. In order to do this, you can follow one of the two methods explained below that we would recommend you to do:

1.   You can merge the scanned images into a Microsoft Word document and create a PDF document. The easiest way to do this is to insert JPEG images into the word document and then create the PDF document while saving the file as explained below:
    *   Click **Insert** on the toolbar at the top of Microsoft Word.
    *   Click **Pictures** and locate the file that you want to add to the document.
    *   Repeat the steps above with all the images that you want to add to the document.
    *   Make sure that all the images are on separate pages.
    *   Click **File** and select **Save As** and select “PDF” as the document type to save.

2.   You can also use Adobe Acrobat to create a PDF.

Alternatively, while scanning the documents, you can opt to create a PDF document and scan the pages to create a single document.

#### What if I have to update my information?

#### What if I have to update my information?

From the **Settings** tab, click **Account info** and then, from the **Seller Account Information** page, click each section that you want to update. If your information has already been verified or the verification process has not yet started, you will be able to update your information. Any updates are subject to verification. Depending on our verification results, you may be asked to provide additional information.

#### Why can't I edit my information?

Information cannot be updated while it is being verified.

#### Can I save my information and complete the form later?

Yes. The Update Wizard will allow you to resume entering data at any step in the process. Make sure that you click **Submit** on each screen after entering your information.

You will still be able to use Seller Central. However, if you do not provide your information within the specified time period, your selling privileges will be suspended. Your privileges may be reinstated once you have entered the requested data and your information have been verified.

#### I provided the information requested. Why has Amazon requested additional information from me?

During the verification process, we may determine that you are required to provide additional information. This information is required to complete your account registration and for safety purposes.

#### How will I be notified if Amazon requires additional information?

If we require additional information, you will be notified via the email address provided at registration as well as via the notification field in Seller Central. You can also see what additional information is required by clicking **Account Info** on the **Settings** tab. Look for the sections highlighted in red.

You will also receive detailed guidance in your [performance notifications](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/performance/notifications).

#### What if my country of residence isn't in the drop-down menu?

If your country of residence is not in the drop-down menu, we don't support that country. Check back often; we're continually adding supported countries.

#### What should I do if I am unable to upload my documents using the tool provided?

Contact [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/hz/contact-us/) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant if you encounter problems while uploading documents.

#### I don't have one of the documents requested. What should I do?

#### I don't have one of the documents requested. What should I do?

If you don’t have one or more of the documents requested, contact [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/cu/contact-us) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant. Make sure that you provide details about the documents you do not have when you contact us or use Amazon's Seller Assistant. We will then get in touch with you regarding alternative documents that we may be able to accept.

#### Why do my documents need to be dated within a certain timeline?

Because Amazon is required by regulation and legislation that identification is done based on up to date information. This information is required to complete your account registration and for safety purposes.

#### What language should I enter the information in?

You can provide the information in Portuguese, Chinese, or English.

## If you are an individual seller

We may ask you to provide proof of address.

**Proof of address**

*   Proof of address must meet the following requirements:
    *   Be dated within the past 180 days
    *   Show the name and the address of the individual
    *   The address entered in Seller Central and the address in the document must match. If the ID document has the address, no additional proof of address is required.
    *   Cannot be handwritten
    *   Cannot be a screenshot

*   Accepted documents include, but are not limited to, the following:
    *   Utility bill (example: water, electricity, gas) in the name of the individual
    *   Bank statement or credit card statement in the name of the individual
    *   Certified Bank Letter in the name of the individual
    *   Income tax bill
    *   Social Security receipt
    *   Bill from estate or property management in the name of the individual
    *   Bank certified customer printout in the name of the individual
    *   Phone bill in the name of the individual
    *   VAT invoice in the name of the individual
**Note:**Documents from financial service providers or online digital banks are acceptable as proof of address from institutions authorized by the PBC Bank of China.

**Identity**

**Identity**

*   Mainland China:
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Valid Residence Identity Card (both sides)
    *   Hukou (pages related to the Business Owner, Legal Representative or Primary Contact, including at least a name, address, ID number)

*   Hong Kong:
    *   Document of Identity for Visa Purposes (Photo and information page)
    *   Hong Kong ID Card (front side) only if the place of birth is Hong Kong, Mainland China, or Macau

*   Taiwan:
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Taiwan Identity Card (front and reverse sides)

## Contact Information

**Information about primary contact person**

The primary contact person (POC) is the main person who has access to the selling account, provides the registration information on behalf of the account holder (the registered seller), and initiates transactions, such as disbursements and refunds. You will be asked to provide contact details of the POC.

Accepted identity documents:

Accepted identity documents:

*   Mainland China
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Valid Residence Identity Card (both sides)
    *   Hukou (pages related to the Business Owner, Legal Representative or Primary Contact, including at least a name, address, ID number)

*   Hong Kong
    *   Document of Identity for Visa Purposes (Photo and information page)
    *   Hong Kong ID Card (front side) only if the place of birth is Hong Kong, Mainland China, or Macau

*   Taiwan
    *   Passport (Photo and information page)
    *   Taiwan Identity Card (front and reverse sides)

**Additional information**

Bank statements from financial service providers or online digital banks are acceptable as proof of address from institutions authorized by the PBC Bank of China.

List: ANNA, Fire, Revolut, Banca Widiba, First Direct, Saxo Payments, Banking Circle, Hype, Selfyconto, CashPlus, Meridan Solutions, Solaris Bank, Chebanca!, Mettle, Starling Bank, Coconut, Monzo, Thinkmoney, CountingUp, N26, Tide, Ebury, Nexi, Webank, EVO Banco, Paysera, Qonto, Fineco, and Penta

**Information about beneficiaries, directors, or trustees**

According to Brazilian regulations, any person holding 25 percent or more of a company's shares or equity is considered an ultimate beneficial owner (UBO). As a result, you may be required to provide information about these individuals. This requirement also extends to trustees and directors.

To verify ownership, you can provide a document that contains the share allotment of the company per owner.

For identification purposes , Amazon may request a passport or Identity Card, along with proof of address documentation.

**Note:**The primary contact person may also be a beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the company.

**Note:**The primary contact person may also be a beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the company.

For more information, see the [Brazil requirements](https://www.gov.br/coaf/pt-br/acesso-a-informacao/Institucional/a-atividade-de-supervisao/regulacao/supervisao/normas-1/resolucao-coaf-no-36-de-10-de-marco-de-2021).

If the appointed POC is a beneficial owner, director, or trustee, but there is another beneficial owner or beneficial owners of the business, you must provide information about all other beneficial owners with 25% or more ownership of the business.

If the primary contact person is not the beneficial owner, director, or trustee of the business, a letter of authorization (LOA) signed by a beneficial owner, director, or trustee may be requested confirming that the primary contact person is authorized to act on behalf of the business. In the case where the POC is the Legal Representative for a seller, a LOA is not required.

## Information Validation

## Information Validation

## How long will it take to verify my information?

Once we receive all of the required information, we will proceed with the verification of your account. Verification could take up to 3 days; however, it may take longer in some situations based on the complexity of the account.

## Am I allowed to sell while my information is being verified?

You will not be able to start selling on Amazon until the information that you have provided has been verified and your account has successfully completed verification.

## How can I appeal Amazon's decision to decline my application?

Verification decisions are final and are based on the information and documentation we have received from you.

## Impact on selling on Amazon

## What happens while my information is being verified?

## Impact on selling on Amazon

## What happens while my information is being verified?

You may be allowed to start selling on Amazon while the information you have provided at registration is being verified, subject to certain limits. Depending on certain criteria, we may require that you provide us with all the required information and that this information is verified before we open your selling account. In this case, you will not be able to start selling on Amazon until the information you have provided is verified and your account has successfully completed verification.

## What happens to my orders if I have not provided the required identity information or if my information is pending verification?

## What happens to my orders if I have not provided the required identity information or if my information is pending verification?

If you have been allowed to start selling on Amazon immediately after registration, you can fulfill your existing orders while your information is pending verification, up to a certain limit. However, you must provide the required identity information as soon as possible, to avoid any constraints regarding your selling account.

## Why has my application for a selling account been declined?

## Why has my application for a selling account been declined?

There are several reasons why we may have declined to open your account. It may be because we were unable to verify your identity as required by applicable legislation. Providing incomplete or inaccurate information is a common reason why verification may not be successful. If you feel your information should have been successfully verified and would like to appeal, [contact Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/contact-us/contact-amazon-form.html) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant.

## What will happen to my FBA inventory?

In the event that you are unable to open your selling account, you can ask to have your inventory returned to you.

## What should I do if I have a document that is not in any of the Amazon-supported Languages?

You have to submit officially translated documents if they are not in any of the Amazon supported languages (Portuguese, English, or Chinese).

You have to submit officially translated documents if they are not in any of the Amazon supported languages (Portuguese, English, or Chinese).

## Why doesn't Amazon Brasil support my country of residence?

Your country of residence may not be currently supported and included in the drop-down list for various reasons, including international restrictions or our ability to process information and documents in specific languages. Check back in the future as Amazon Brasil is continuously expanding the list of supported countries.

## Does Amazon have the right to change the agreement without notice?

We are committed to maintaining a dynamic, safe and compliant store for both customers and sellers. The changes to our agreements are a necessary step toward this goal. Therefore, we are requesting that all sellers accept the agreements so that we may continue to offer both customers and sellers outstanding products and services.

## What does the Business Solutions Agreement cover?

## What does the Business Solutions Agreement cover?

The Business Solutions Agreement contains the terms and conditions for selling on Amazon, Fulfillment by Amazon, Services, and Product Ads.

## I do not want to accept the new agreements. Who can I contact to discuss changes?

The agreements have to be accepted as written. Our goal is to have a transparent service that works for both sellers and Amazon.

If you have questions on the overall process, we encourage you to contact Selling Partner Support or use Amazon's Seller Assistant, but be aware that they will be unable to waive any terms of the agreements.
